text: The surgeon makes a circumferential incision on the rectal mucosa, one centimeter above the dentate line, carefully cutting through the muscular layer to preserve tissue for anastomosis while avoiding bleeding or injury.
The surgeon places sutures to suspend the anal canal, providing stabilization during the procedure.
The surgeon completes the mobilization of the rectum, returning it to its normal position. The mesorectum is then ligated and divided using an energy device at the predetermined resection margin.
The surgeon cuts the colon from one side and secures it with a suture to prevent retraction into the abdomen. The resection is then completed.
The surgeon performs a single-layer anastomosis using separate sutures, carefully aligning the rectal tissue to ensure a secure connection.
